title: 怪屋
summary:
During the end of the war in Egypt, Charles met Sophia Leontidis, a highly efficient twenty-two-year-old manager at a Foreign Ministry outpost. As they became close friends, Charles realized he loved her and intended to marry her upon his return to England. During a dinner at the Shepherd's restaurant, Charles expressed his intentions but declined to propose immediately, wishing to allow Sophia the freedom to assess the post-war situation independently. Sophia agreed, noting that Charles did not truly know her. She revealed she lived in a "weird little cottage" in Swindon with an extended family, including her grandfather, Aristide Leonidis, a wealthy Greek man from Smyrna.

Two years later, Charles returned to England. After landing, he read an obituary in *The Times* announcing the sudden death of Aristide Leonidis at his home in Swinley. Charles found the repetitive nature of the obituary announcements strange. He sent a telegram to Sophia expressing his condolences, and she replied that they would proceed with their planned dinner at Mario's restaurant. When they met, Sophia was dressed in prominent black mourning attire. Although they initially bypassed their awkwardness by reminiscing about their time in Cairo, Charles felt a growing sense of unease, sensing that something was not right regarding the circumstances of her grandfather's death or Sophia's calm demeanor.
